framework_bugs.summary: upgrade-project.sh emits 'cp: ... identical' warning when refreshing helper scripts on already-current project
framework_bugs.reproduction: bash scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-production on a freshly-init'd sponsored_poc project (helper scripts already at framework version)
framework_bugs.expected: Silent no-op when source and dest are identical, OR an idempotent copy pattern (e.g. compare-then-copy) without surfacing cp's diagnostic to stderr
framework_bugs.actual: Output ends with 'cp: scripts/pending-approval.sh and <project>/scripts/pending-approval.sh are identical (not copied).'  Cosmetic; appears at end of an otherwise successful run.

tests.name: no_uncommitted_secrets
tests.details: Refined grep '(password|api[_-]?key|secret|token)\s*[:=]\s*"...{16+}' returned only example placeholders in docs/reference/builders-guide.md and cli-setup-addendum.md (BITBUCKET_APP_PASSWORD="your-app-password") — no real credentials

summary: Upgrade sponsored_poc/mobile/light -> production succeeded end-to-end; all PR #17/#18/#19 fixes confirmed (U-A non-interactive init, U-B fake-remote tolerance, U-E poc_mode lookup, U-F sentinel respect, U-H helper copy, U-N/U-O framework-self guard); single Low-severity cosmetic finding (cp 'identical' warning during helper refresh).
